note: Independent local-Opus evaluator scores (blind to source) over the generations in local-opus-generations.json. Blind labels S1-S4 mapped back to set_ids. Purpose: validate that the rubric DISCRIMINATES (weak fixture must score far below the strong sets) before it judges real M2.7/M3 outputs. NOT an M2.7/M3 result.

weak-fixture-T1.rationale: Vague non-tests ('look at the errors'); both hypotheses restate 'PR is low'; no mechanism; no separating observable; no theorem named.

opus-T1.rationale: Cauchy-relation/EAM-embedding, Brown-Sethna sloppiness, and strain-basis rank are real, distinct mechanisms with matched-cohort tests and clean separators (Cauchy pressure, Fisher ratio, elastic-constant count); targets the PR<2 bound with an explicit falsifier.

opus-T3.rationale: Jacobian-rank vs observable-rank vs Born-stability-softening are genuinely different correct mechanisms; autodiff-rank / observable-count / min-Born-eigenvalue separate them; targets the open PR<=rank conjecture with a single-crossing falsifier.

opus-T4.rationale: Bonding-type offset vs energy/mechanical orthogonality vs curvature-regularisation make divergent sign-flip predictions with distinct descriptors; bounds the open distill-beats-baseline claim.

conclusion: Rubric discriminates cleanly: weak fixture 0/10 vs strong sets 10/10. The evaluator + rubric are validated and ready to judge real MiniMax-M2.7 vs M3 generations once a key is present.
